rulings.Case Title: TERRY BROWN ET AL VS. WALGREENS BOOTS ALLIANCE, INC, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An In-Camera Hearing To Determine The Privileged San Francisco Police Department Investigative Records That Must Be Released To Plaintiffs Pursuant To The Courts Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS TERRY BROWN AS CO-SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DECEDENT CHYNNA BROWN (A.K.A. BANKO BROWN), AND KEVINISHA HENDERSON's Motion For An In-Camera Hearing To Determine The Privileged San Francisco Police Department Investigative Records That Must Be Released To Plaintiffs Pursuant To The Courts Protective Order. Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant the motion but wishes to discuss scheduling and logistics for the in camera review with the parties. SFPD's counsel should be present for the hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AIMEE SILVER VS. GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order For Deposition Against Defendant Greystar
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025, Line 2. 1 - PLAINTIFF AIMEE SILVER's Motion For Protective Order For Deposition Against Defendant Greystar. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part. A court on a motion for protective order may, "for good cause shown, . . . make any order that justice requires to protect any party . . . from unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense." Accommodation for a disability is good cause, but the court may not make accommodations that "fundamentally alter the nature of the service, program, or activity" for which accommodations are sought. (Rule of Court 1.100(f)(3).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set One

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order For Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Production, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025, Line 2. 3 - PLAINTIFF AIMEE SILVER's Motion For Protective Order For Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Production, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One. Plaintiff Aimee Silver seeks a protective order providing her with an extension of time to August 1, 2025 to provide amended responses to defendant Greystar California Inc.'s written discovery, to wit Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One. The court also has before it Greystar's motion to compel Silver's further responses to this discovery. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the protective order motion in part and denies it in part. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) rulings.Case Title: ZARINA OSTAPOVA ET AL VS. MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. In this pleadings motion, all parties who have been served and have appeared in the case request that the court grant leave for plaintiffs to file a Second Amended Complaint as stipulated to by appearing parties pursuant to the Galek Declaration, paragraph 14, and the Cohen Declaration, paragraph 13. The court grants this alternative relief. Plaintiffs shall file within 10 court days of entry of this order. The motion to strike the prayer for punitive and exemplary damages from the soon-to-be-superseded First Amended Complaint is denied as moot. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-25-621796 WITH CASE CGC-25-621771
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025, Line 7. DEFENDANTS JONNA CORPORATION, INC.
